# 40 Mock Vs Actual Contract Delta

        - Task: `seq_040`
        - Visual mode: `Contract_Cockpit`

        ## Mock_now_execution

        The mock-now lane is not a temporary prose note. It is the executable contract that current simulators, current projections, and current UX states must implement right now.

        | Dependency | Simulator Counterpart | Mock Default | Actual Provider Variability |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `dep_nhs_login_rail` | NHS login auth and session twin (proof_twin, hybrid_contract_twin) | Freeze the route in claim-pending or auth-read-only posture until writable authority is re-proven for the same subject and route intent. | Client identifiers and signing material<br>Environment-scoped redirect URIs<br>Official branding or consent-page chrome outside Vecells control |
| `dep_pds_fhir_enrichment` | Optional PDS enrichment twin (workflow_twin, permanent_fallback) | Keep enrichment fully off and rely on local matching only; do not widen patient identity confidence from incomplete or legally unapproved PDS signals. | Access mode and network path<br>Certificate or credential profile<br>Demographic field mapping breadth |
| `dep_telephony_ivr_recording_provider` | Telephony and IVR twin (fault_injection_twin, hybrid_contract_twin) | Hold the request in callback, audio-review, or urgent-live-only posture when recording availability is weaker than evidence readiness. | Number inventory and vendor account identifiers<br>Webhook signatures and callback payload shape<br>Recording retrieval mechanics |
| `dep_transcription_processing_provider` | Transcription processing twin (fault_injection_twin, hybrid_contract_twin) | Keep transcript output in degraded, manual-review, or continuation-challenge posture until readiness is explicitly safety-usable. | Latency profile and callback shape<br>Vocabulary or confidence metadata<br>Storage scope for raw transcript artifacts |
| `dep_sms_notification_provider` | Support replay and resend twin (proof_twin, permanent_fallback)<br>SMS delivery twin (workflow_twin, hybrid_contract_twin) | Withdraw seeded continuation trust and fall back to challenge-based continuation or another governed contact route when recipient or delivery truth is disputed. | Callback payload details used during replay review<br>Provider-side event identifiers<br>Export format used for post-incident audit pulls<br>Sender ID format<br>Provider delivery callback schema<br>Link delivery TTL values |
| `dep_email_notification_provider` | Support replay and resend twin (proof_twin, permanent_fallback)<br>Email notification twin (workflow_twin, hybrid_contract_twin) | Preserve a read-only, pending-delivery, or repair-needed posture until current delivery evidence or a governed fallback settles the route. | Callback payload details used during replay review<br>Provider-side event identifiers<br>Export format used for post-incident audit pulls<br>Sender and domain configuration<br>Webhook payload signatures<br>Provider event vocabularies for bounce and suppression |
| `dep_malware_scanning_provider` | Malware and artifact scanning twin (fault_injection_twin, hybrid_contract_twin) | Fail closed into quarantined, unreadable, or reacquire-required posture until the current artifact verdict is explicit. | Verdict taxonomy and confidence annotations<br>Event callback or polling shape<br>Quarantine storage integration details |
| `dep_im1_pairing_programme` | IM1 principal-system EMIS twin (near-live_contract_twin, hybrid_contract_twin)<br>IM1 principal-system TPP twin (near-live_contract_twin, hybrid_contract_twin) | Keep the path simulator-only, supported-test-only, or assisted-only until the exact supplier and environment clear the IM1 stage pack. | Exact field map and endpoint tuples<br>Supplier-specific manage coverage<br>Search or hold feature flags by supplier estate |
| `dep_gp_system_supplier_paths` | IM1 principal-system EMIS twin (near-live_contract_twin, hybrid_contract_twin)<br>IM1 principal-system TPP twin (near-live_contract_twin, hybrid_contract_twin) | Keep supplier paths in simulator, assisted-only, or reconciliation-required posture until the current supplier binding and supported action tuple are published. | Exact field map and endpoint tuples<br>Supplier-specific manage coverage<br>Search or hold feature flags by supplier estate |
| `dep_local_booking_supplier_adapters` | Booking provider confirmation twin (near-live_contract_twin, hybrid_contract_twin) | Hold the booking in confirmation-pending, supplier-reconciliation-pending, waitlist, or callback fallback posture until durable confirmation proof exists. | Supplier capability tuple and surface naming<br>Commit timing windows<br>Practice-facing receipt or acknowledgement details |
| `dep_network_capacity_partner_feeds` | Booking capacity feed twin (workflow_twin, hybrid_contract_twin) | Treat the feed as diagnostic-only, callback-only, or quarantined when freshness or trust admission no longer holds. | Feed transport details<br>Capacity refresh cadence<br>Partner-specific no-slot reason vocabularies |
| `dep_cross_org_secure_messaging_mesh` | MESH message path twin (near-live_contract_twin, hybrid_contract_twin) | Keep the route in pending-delivery, monitored-mailbox, or escalation posture when transport receipt is weaker than current delivery evidence. | Mailbox identifiers and owner ODS values<br>Route manager mode and queue wiring<br>Exact callback or polling strategy |
| `dep_origin_practice_ack_rail` | Booking provider confirmation twin (near-live_contract_twin, hybrid_contract_twin) | Keep practice visibility in overdue-ack, recovery-required, or exception-pending posture until the current truth tuple is acknowledged or explicitly excepted. | Supplier capability tuple and surface naming<br>Commit timing windows<br>Practice-facing receipt or acknowledgement details |
| `dep_pharmacy_directory_dohs` | Pharmacy directory and choice twin (proof_twin, replace_with_live_guarded) | Regenerate the directory, warn about drift, or constrain to clinician-guided choice when the current snapshot is stale or materially changed. | Directory API version and query parameters<br>Provider capability filter sets<br>Source-specific freshness metadata |
| `dep_pharmacy_referral_transport` | Pharmacy dispatch transport twin (near-live_contract_twin, hybrid_contract_twin) | Hold the referral in proof-pending, contradiction-review, or controlled-redispatch posture until current dispatch proof and acknowledgement thresholds are satisfied. | Transport channel and receipt shape<br>Mailbox or endpoint identifiers<br>Provider-specific retry window values |
| `dep_pharmacy_outcome_observation` | Pharmacy visibility and Update Record twin (proof_twin, hybrid_contract_twin) | Keep the case in outcome-reconciliation-pending or safety-review posture until the outcome matches the active case and replay concerns are cleared. | Transport wrapper and acknowledgement cadence<br>Supplier-specific visibility field mapping<br>Assured-combination registry values |
| `dep_pharmacy_urgent_return_professional_routes` | No current simulator counterpart; explicit watch-only contract pack only | Treat the route as unavailable or unsafe until urgent-return ownership and current acknowledgement are explicit. | Monitored mailbox or professional-number identifiers<br>Practice-level rota and escalation ownership details<br>Route acknowledgement wording and local handoff cadence |
| `dep_nhs_app_embedded_channel_ecosystem` | NHS App embedded bridge twin (workflow_twin, replace_with_live_guarded) | Drop to safe browser handoff, read-only embed, or placeholder-only posture when manifest, bridge, or embedded continuity evidence drifts. | Site-link values and package identifiers<br>JS bridge surface details<br>Environment-specific jump-off inventory |
| `dep_assistive_model_vendor_family` | No current simulator counterpart; explicit watch-only contract pack only | Force assistive capability back to shadow-only, observe-only, provenance-only, or hidden posture whenever the trust envelope is degraded, stale, or quarantined. | Vendor scoring and inference metadata shape<br>Subprocessor inventory and region posture<br>Drafting or summarisation surface details that remain behind adapter boundaries |
| `dep_nhs_assurance_and_standards_sources` | No current simulator counterpart; explicit watch-only contract pack only | Freeze the affected onboarding, release, or provider assumption pack until the current standards baseline is rechecked and mapped. | Source-link locations and naming changes<br>Supplier-assurance attachment formats<br>Review cadence for refreshed or retired guidance sources |

        ## Actual_provider_strategy_later

        The actual-provider lane upgrades evidence and adapter wiring, not the business truth contract. Any future provider delta must stay within the envelope below or be recorded as an explicit conflict.

        | Dependency | Upgrade Evidence | Non-Negotiables | Explicit Override Rule |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `dep_nhs_login_rail` | Partner approval and current redirect inventory<br>Technical conformance evidence<br>Environment-specific callback and session-parity rehearsal<br>Fresh gate snapshot stamped with the capture date<br>Official label checks still match the current NHS login guidance<br>Named redirect review reference linked to the active route family<br>Scope approval reference tied to the same environment target | Callback success alone never equals writable authority<br>Claim-pending and auth-read-only remain separate post-auth states<br>Route-intent binding remains mandatory for write access | Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Client identifiers and signing material, Environment-scoped redirect URIs, Official branding or consent-page chrome outside Vecells control; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout. |
| `dep_pds_fhir_enrichment` | Legal basis and selected access mode<br>Named approver and environment<br>Wrong-patient and rollback readiness evidence<br>Draft receipt linked to the chosen access mode<br>Current hazard and risk log references attached to the same draft<br>Named legal or governance approval reference<br>Rollback rehearsal evidence tied to the same route-bound access mode | PDS stays optional and feature-flagged<br>Local binding and NHS login remain the baseline identity authority<br>Wrong-patient repair holds remain explicit even when enrichment is enabled | Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Access mode and network path, Certificate or credential profile, Demographic field mapping breadth; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout. |
| `dep_telephony_ivr_recording_provider` | Vendor approval and spend authority<br>Webhook security and replay posture<br>Recording policy approval plus named environment<br>Draft receipt for the chosen vendor and number profile<br>Current recording policy and webhook security refs attached to the same draft<br>Provider number or purchase receipt<br>Spend approval reference and named approver tied to the same action | Webhook success never equals clinically usable evidence by itself<br>Urgent-live preemption outranks routine continuation<br>Recording availability stays weaker than evidence readiness | Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Number inventory and vendor account identifiers, Webhook signatures and callback payload shape, Recording retrieval mechanics; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout. |
| `dep_transcription_processing_provider` | Region and retention posture<br>Webhook security evidence<br>Named approver and final operator acknowledgement<br>Draft receipt for the chosen provider and project scope<br>Current region, retention, storage, and quarantine references<br>Vault receipt for the webhook secret reference<br>Current replay-control and callback security evidence | Transcript output remains derivative, not source truth<br>Manual review remains explicit when coverage is inadequate<br>Superseded transcript runs stay visible and replayable | Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Latency profile and callback shape, Vocabulary or confidence metadata, Storage scope for raw transcript artifacts; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout. |
| `dep_sms_notification_provider` | Repair policy approval<br>Audit log export and replay guard evidence<br>Human-only checkpoint policy from seq_039<br>SMS sender ownership<br>Wrong-recipient repair controls<br>Spend and environment approval<br>Project draft receipt for the chosen provider family<br>Current repair, migration, and scope references bound to the same draft<br>Ownership or DNS handoff reference<br>Signed webhook security reference for the same sender or domain identity | Controlled resend always stays explicit and audited<br>Support replay never bypasses repair windows or scope fences<br>Disputed delivery remains a first-class blocker<br>Continuation grants remain bounded<br>Delivery ambiguity and wrong-recipient suspicion remain explicit<br>Redemption stays fenced by the active subject and context | Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Callback payload details used during replay review, Provider-side event identifiers, Export format used for post-incident audit pulls, Sender ID format, Provider delivery callback schema, Link delivery TTL values; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout. |
| `dep_email_notification_provider` | Repair policy approval<br>Audit log export and replay guard evidence<br>Human-only checkpoint policy from seq_039<br>Sender ownership and verified domain<br>Webhook security evidence<br>Project scope and named environment approval<br>Project draft receipt for the chosen provider family<br>Current repair, migration, and scope references bound to the same draft<br>Ownership or DNS handoff reference<br>Signed webhook security reference for the same sender or domain identity | Controlled resend always stays explicit and audited<br>Support replay never bypasses repair windows or scope fences<br>Disputed delivery remains a first-class blocker<br>Delivery acceptance never equals patient-visible truth by itself<br>Repair and resend remain governed product actions<br>Template version binding stays explicit | Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Callback payload details used during replay review, Provider-side event identifiers, Export format used for post-incident audit pulls, Sender and domain configuration, Webhook payload signatures, Provider event vocabularies for bounce and suppression; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout. |
| `dep_malware_scanning_provider` | Storage scope and quarantine policy<br>Webhook security and mutation gate posture<br>Named approver and operator acknowledgement<br>Draft receipt for the chosen provider and project scope<br>Current region, retention, storage, and quarantine references<br>Vault receipt for the webhook secret reference<br>Current replay-control and callback security evidence | Suspicious and quarantined remain separate outcomes<br>Unreadable artifacts never silently pass<br>Manual release from quarantine remains explicit and auditable | Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Verdict taxonomy and confidence annotations, Event callback or polling shape, Quarantine storage integration details; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout. |
| `dep_im1_pairing_programme` | Current supplier roster and pairing approval<br>Bounded use-case approval<br>Named sponsor, approver, and environment<br>Fresh path evidence snapshot<br>Official supplier labels still match the current provider guidance<br>Draft checkpoint receipt tied to the active supplier pair<br>Current capability and architecture evidence refs attached to the same draft | Supplier capability matrices remain explicit<br>Hold expiry and commit ambiguity stay separate from calm confirmation<br>Local waitlist and fallback law remain core-owned | Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Exact field map and endpoint tuples, Supplier-specific manage coverage, Search or hold feature flags by supplier estate; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout. |
| `dep_gp_system_supplier_paths` | Current supplier roster and pairing approval<br>Bounded use-case approval<br>Named sponsor, approver, and environment<br>Fresh path evidence snapshot<br>Official supplier labels still match the current provider guidance<br>Draft checkpoint receipt tied to the active supplier pair<br>Current capability and architecture evidence refs attached to the same draft | Supplier capability matrices remain explicit<br>Hold expiry and commit ambiguity stay separate from calm confirmation<br>Local waitlist and fallback law remain core-owned | Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Exact field map and endpoint tuples, Supplier-specific manage coverage, Search or hold feature flags by supplier estate; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout. |
| `dep_local_booking_supplier_adapters` | Bounded booking MVP and architecture refresh<br>Named sponsor plus commissioning posture<br>Practice acknowledgement route evidence<br>Fresh path evidence snapshot<br>Official supplier labels still match the current provider guidance<br>Draft checkpoint receipt tied to the active supplier pair<br>Current capability and architecture evidence refs attached to the same draft | Acceptance never outruns confirmation truth<br>Practice acknowledgement remains separate from patient reassurance<br>Manual callback fallback remains explicit when proof is incomplete | Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Supplier capability tuple and surface naming, Commit timing windows, Practice-facing receipt or acknowledgement details; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout. |
| `dep_network_capacity_partner_feeds` | Named partner feed provenance<br>Freshness and expiry policy<br>Callback fallback ownership evidence<br>Fresh path evidence snapshot<br>Official supplier labels still match the current provider guidance<br>Draft checkpoint receipt tied to the active supplier pair<br>Current capability and architecture evidence refs attached to the same draft | No-slot and callback-required remain explicit states<br>Freshness proof stays visible to operators<br>Capacity feed updates never erase confirmation truth gates | Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Feed transport details, Capacity refresh cadence, Partner-specific no-slot reason vocabularies; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout. |
| `dep_cross_org_secure_messaging_mesh` | Named ODS owner and manager mode<br>API onboarding completion or approved live path<br>Minimum-necessary review plus named approver<br>Application draft receipt linked to the mailbox row<br>Current owner ODS and minimum-necessary review refs on the same draft<br>Workflow request or mailbox ticket id<br>Named approver and environment target bound to the same attempt | Transport receipt remains supporting evidence, not business truth<br>Replay fences stay mandatory<br>Escalation behavior remains explicit when mailbox or secure transport degrades | Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Mailbox identifiers and owner ODS values, Route manager mode and queue wiring, Exact callback or polling strategy; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout. |
| `dep_origin_practice_ack_rail` | Bounded booking MVP and architecture refresh<br>Named sponsor plus commissioning posture<br>Practice acknowledgement route evidence<br>Fresh path evidence snapshot<br>Official supplier labels still match the current provider guidance<br>Draft checkpoint receipt tied to the active supplier pair<br>Current capability and architecture evidence refs attached to the same draft<br>Application draft receipt linked to the mailbox row<br>Current owner ODS and minimum-necessary review refs on the same draft<br>Workflow request or mailbox ticket id<br>Named approver and environment target bound to the same attempt | Acceptance never outruns confirmation truth<br>Practice acknowledgement remains separate from patient reassurance<br>Manual callback fallback remains explicit when proof is incomplete | Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Supplier capability tuple and surface naming, Commit timing windows, Practice-facing receipt or acknowledgement details; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout. |
| `dep_pharmacy_directory_dohs` | Service Search access approval and route policy<br>Choice tuple freshness and capability evidence<br>Current environment-specific directory entitlement<br>Fresh route snapshot showing the current preferred, fallback, and manual paths<br>Official label checks still match current pharmacy guidance<br>Named transport route tuple or provider receipt<br>Current dispatch proof thresholds and approver evidence bound to the same attempt | Choice proof binds the exact directory tuple<br>Directory lookup never equals dispatch or closure proof<br>Refresh and no-safe-provider states remain first-class | Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Directory API version and query parameters, Provider capability filter sets, Source-specific freshness metadata; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout. |
| `dep_pharmacy_referral_transport` | Named transport route profile and provider tuple<br>Dispatch proof and acknowledgement thresholds signed off<br>Manual urgent-return ownership rehearsal evidence<br>Fresh route snapshot showing the current preferred, fallback, and manual paths<br>Official label checks still match current pharmacy guidance<br>Named transport route tuple or provider receipt<br>Current dispatch proof thresholds and approver evidence bound to the same attempt | Dispatch proof never equals settled referral truth on acceptance alone<br>Acknowledgement and expiry remain separate facts<br>Manual urgent-return remains first-class even after live transport exists | Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Transport channel and receipt shape, Mailbox or endpoint identifiers, Provider-specific retry window values; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout. |
| `dep_pharmacy_outcome_observation` | Assured supplier/system combination for Update Record<br>Reconciliation runtime implementation reference<br>Watch-register closure for visibility and manual fallback gaps<br>Fresh route snapshot showing the current preferred, fallback, and manual paths<br>Official label checks still match current pharmacy guidance<br>Named transport route tuple or provider receipt<br>Current dispatch proof thresholds and approver evidence bound to the same attempt | Update Record is visibility only, never urgent transport<br>Weak or delayed outcomes never auto-close the request lineage<br>Practice-disabled fallback remains explicit and same-shell | Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Transport wrapper and acknowledgement cadence, Supplier-specific visibility field mapping, Assured-combination registry values; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout. |
| `dep_pharmacy_urgent_return_professional_routes` | Fresh route snapshot showing the current preferred, fallback, and manual paths<br>Official label checks still match current pharmacy guidance<br>Named transport route tuple or provider receipt<br>Current dispatch proof thresholds and approver evidence bound to the same attempt | Urgent return remains a human-operated safety net and cannot collapse into ordinary Update Record semantics<br>Acknowledgement on the current route is required before the case can look settled<br>Phone escalation remains explicit when the monitored digital path is unavailable | Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Monitored mailbox or professional-number identifiers, Practice-level rota and escalation ownership details, Route acknowledgement wording and local handoff cadence; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout. |
| `dep_nhs_app_embedded_channel_ecosystem` | Scope-window approval and commissioning posture<br>Current NHS login readiness for embedded use<br>Named environment, approver, and release evidence<br>Draft receipt for the current NHS App stage pack<br>Current NHS login readiness, eligibility, and demo references<br>Named commissioning or sponsor approval reference<br>Current accessibility, design, and service evidence refs<br>Fresh gate snapshot stamped with the capture date<br>Official label checks still match the current NHS login guidance<br>Named redirect review reference linked to the active route family<br>Scope approval reference tied to the same environment target | Embedded return intent remains explicit<br>Header suppression and webview constraints remain testable<br>Site-link publication stays environment-scoped and mutation-gated | Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Site-link values and package identifiers, JS bridge surface details, Environment-specific jump-off inventory; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout. |
| `dep_assistive_model_vendor_family` | Current supplier assurance pack for the exact route family and cohort<br>Rollback rehearsal and kill-switch proof tied to the proposed rollout tuple<br>Named approver, live cohort definition, and trust-envelope freshness | Assistive vendors remain optional and may not become a baseline correctness dependency<br>Shadow-only, observe-only, hidden, or frozen states remain first-class outcomes<br>Human provenance and rollback paths stay explicit even when a vendor is healthy | Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Vendor scoring and inference metadata shape, Subprocessor inventory and region posture, Drafting or summarisation surface details that remain behind adapter boundaries; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout. |
| `dep_nhs_assurance_and_standards_sources` | Current standards version map for the release or onboarding pack under review<br>Governance acknowledgement that refreshed standards baselines were rechecked<br>Evidence-pack refresh proving release assumptions still match the current watchlist | Release and onboarding assumptions must stay bound to the current standards watchlist<br>Stale or moved standards sources block widening until reviewed explicitly<br>Standards exceptions remain explicit and time-bounded rather than implied | Provider-specific differences may only alter adapter-facing details such as Source-link locations and naming changes, Supplier-assurance attachment formats, Review cadence for refreshed or retired guidance sources; any change to proof thresholds, ambiguity boundaries, degraded posture, manual fallback, or audience posture requires a conflict-register entry before rollout. |

        Delta rules:

        - Live providers may upgrade evidence quality and reduce ambiguity, but they may not redefine what counts as proof.
        - Manual fallback remains available even after a provider goes live if the contract row says continuity still requires it.
        - Simulator retirement is never implied by live onboarding; replacement mode stays explicit per dependency row.
